title: Magnetic Reed Switch Door Position Sensor
design_question: How to detect whether a garage door or cabinet lid is fully open or fully closed using two discrete position states?
selected_approach: Two reed switch pairs (surface-mount, N/O): one at the fully-closed position, one at the fully-open position. Magnets on moving door panel; sensors on door frame. Each sensor connected to ESP32 GPIO with 10 kΩ pull-up to 3.3 V; LOW = magnet present = position reached. Interrupt-on-change detects transition < 5 ms.
rationale: Reed switches require no power when static and survive ≥ 10⁷ operations — adequate for 10 open/close cycles per day for 27+ years. N/O contacts make wiring failures (open circuit) detectable as 'door not at limit', the safer default. No alignment sensitivity beyond ±5 mm operating distance.

alternatives.reason_rejected: Requires power; more complex than a passive reed switch for a simple open/closed application.
Sensitive to ambient light and surface reflectivity; fails in dusty garage environment.

verification_notes: FACT: 'Magnetic Reed Switch for Contact/Position Detection' confirms N/O contact, 10⁷–10⁸ cycle rating, and 10–20 mm operating distance.
supporting_fact_titles: Magnetic Reed Switch for Contact/Position Detection
